title: Full-stack fast-track curriculum
subtitle: Spring Boot 4 · React · MySQL — beginner to job-ready

infoBoxes.heading: Total duration
infoBoxes.text: 20–25 weeks of self-paced learning · ~2 hrs/day · 5 days/week

infoBoxes.heading: What you'll build
infoBoxes.text: A full e-commerce platform with React storefront, Spring Boot REST API, MySQL database, JWT authentication, and cloud deployment — usable as a real portfolio project.

overview.tip: Click any phase card above to see the detailed curriculum. Each phase ends with a mini-project so you always have something to show.

phases.title: Foundations

project.heading: Phase 1 project
project.text: Build a simple student grade calculator: Java CLI app that saves/reads records to MySQL using raw JDBC.

phases.tip: Don't skip Java basics — Spring Boot's magic makes no sense without understanding classes and interfaces first. Spend at least 2 weeks here.
phases.askPrompt: Give me a detailed week-by-week study plan for Phase 1 of my full-stack curriculum — Java, SQL, JavaScript, and dev tools setup

phases.title: Backend with Spring Boot 4

topics.heading: REST API
items: HTTP methods: GET POST PUT DELETE

project.heading: Phase 2 project
project.text: Build a Product Catalog REST API: CRUD for products and categories, JWT auth, pagination — testable in Postman.

phases.tip: Test every endpoint in Postman as you build it. Don't wait until the end — broken APIs are much harder to debug later.
phases.askPrompt: Give me a detailed week-by-week study plan for Phase 2 — Spring Boot 4 REST API development with MySQL and Spring Security

phases.title: Frontend with React

topics.heading: React Router
items: Setting up routes with React Router v7

topics.heading: State & data
items: TanStack Query (React Query) for API calls

project.heading: Phase 3 project
project.text: Build a product browsing UI: list view, detail page, search filter, cart state — all with mock JSON data (no backend yet).

phases.tip: Use TanStack Query from the start — it replaces 80% of the useEffect + fetch boilerplate you'd otherwise write manually.
phases.askPrompt: Give me a detailed week-by-week study plan for Phase 3 — React latest with React Router, TanStack Query, and Tailwind CSS

phases.title: Full-stack integration

topics.heading: API integration
items: Connect React to Spring Boot API
Axios vs fetch — when to use each

topics.heading: Auth flow
items: Storing JWT in memory / httpOnly cookie

topics.heading: File uploads
items: Multipart file upload in Spring Boot
Store images on disk or cloud (S3/R2)
Return image URL in API response

topics.heading: Deployment
items: Deploy Spring Boot to Railway / Render

project.heading: Phase 4 project
project.text: Launch your full e-commerce app live: React frontend + Spring Boot API + MySQL, with user auth, product CRUD, and a working cart — deployed and accessible via URL.

phases.tip: Deploy early — even a half-finished app. Deployment surprises (env vars, CORS, DB connections) are easier to fix incrementally.
phases.askPrompt: Give me a detailed week-by-week plan for Phase 4 — connecting React to Spring Boot, JWT auth flow, and deploying to Railway + Vercel

phases.title: Advanced topics

project.heading: Phase 5 capstone
project.text: Polish your e-commerce app: add tests, fix N+1 queries, add Flyway migrations, set up GitHub Actions CI — then put it on your portfolio/resume.

phases.tip: Testing and migrations are what separate hobbyist code from professional code. Clients and employers notice the difference immediately.
phases.askPrompt: Give me a detailed week-by-week plan for Phase 5 — testing, performance, dev practices, and Docker for my Spring Boot + React app
